AAPL.title: Sandisk (SNDK) Stock Jumps 11% After Apple Flags Unavoidable Memory Price Hikes
AAPL.summary: Apple confirmed rising memory costs, pointing to supplier pricing leverage that affects upcoming products. Following these comments, Sandisk (NasdaqGS:SNDK) saw its stock surge about 11% as investors reacted to the pricing signal. Apple's move comes alongside Intel's agreement to manufacture Apple-designed AI semiconductors, reinforcing demand for high-performance memory. The current Sandisk share price is about $2,182.451, with the stock up 16% over the past week and 57.8% over the past...

INTC.title: Intel taps industry veteran Seok-Hee Lee to lead foundry packaging push
INTC.summary: Intel on Thursday named Seok-Hee Lee executive vice president of its contract chip-manufacturing division ‌as it sharpens focus on its advanced packaging business.  The ‌American chipmaker has been trying to reinvigorate its manufacturing business under CEO ​Lip-Bu Tan, after missing out on the AI boom.  The appointment follows President Donald Trump's announcement earlier in the day that Apple had agreed to work with Intel to design and manufacture ‌its chips in the ⁠U.S. in a boost to the chipmaker's contract manufacturing business.

INTC.title: Why Is Intel (INTC) Stock Rocketing Higher Today
INTC.summary: Shares of computer processor maker Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) jumped 10.4% in the afternoon session after President Trump posted on Truth Social that "Apple agreed to work with Intel to design and build its Chips in America."

INTC.title: Trump Just Sent Intel Stock Soaring. Here's Why.
INTC.summary: Intel stock rallies as President Donald Trump says Apple has partnered with its foundry division to make chips domestically. But Wall Street remains cautious on INTC shares.

WDC.title: Western Digital (WDC) Is Up 45.3% After AI Storage Momentum and Rising Memory Prices - Has The Bull Case Changed?
WDC.summary: In recent days, Western Digital has been at the center of a wave of positive research updates, product showcases for AI data centers, and stronger earnings, all tied to surging demand for high-capacity storage and memory. This momentum has been reinforced by easing geopolitical risks, industry-wide optimism on AI infrastructure, and confirmation from major customers like Apple that memory price increases are becoming unavoidable.
